free_text: Calculation of photon production cross sections in
In connection with interregional project INT/1/018
Cross sections have been calculated with EMPIRE code
accounts for the evaporation of neutrons, protons and
alphas, as well as for the preequilibrium emission
of nucleons. Full gamma de-excitation cascades
between and from the continuum of states and from
individual low lying levels were considered. Compound
nucleus contribution was calculated according to the
Hauser-Feshbach formulation, with level densities by
Gilbert and Cameron, parameterised by Reffo, partial
wave transmission coefficients calculated from optical
and gamma-cascades assuming E1,E2 and M1 transitions,
Calculation of the precompound contribution was based
on the geometry dependent hybrid model, with the
Both the precompound and compound nucleus components
in some cases (n,np),(n,an). the neutron capture
gamma-spectra from compound nucleus have been found
negligible at 14.5 MeV. Semi-direct component due to
neutron capture was not accounted for.
As much as 50% of the integrated gamma-ray production
cross section corresponds to low energy transitions
between the individual levels. The average
multiplicity OF these gamma-rays is about 2 but it
fluctuates heavily depending on the decay scheme, or
on the knowledge of it. This causes mainly the
differences between the gamma-ray production cross
A minor source of uncertainty is the omission of weak
reaction channel in the calculation. The channels
omitted contributed to no more than 30 mb of particle
cross section each. The resulting underestimation of
the gamma-ray production cross section does not exceed
300 mb, which is insignificant with respect to the
amplitude of the fluctuations mentioned above.
